questions.prompt: For \(r(x)=\frac{(x-2)(x+1)}{(x-2)(x-4)}\), which statement is true?

choices.label: A
choices.text: There is a vertical asymptote at \(x=2\) and a hole at \(x=4\).

choices.label: B
choices.text: There is a hole at \(x=2\) and a vertical asymptote at \(x=4\).

choices.label: C
choices.text: There are vertical asymptotes at both \(x=2\) and \(x=4\).

choices.label: D
choices.text: There are holes at both \(x=2\) and \(x=4\).

choices.label: E
choices.text: The function is continuous for all real x.

questions.explanation: The factor \(x-2\) cancels, creating a removable discontinuity (hole) at 2. The uncanceled denominator factor \(x-4\) creates a vertical asymptote.

questions.prompt:
A particle moves along the x-axis in such a way that its acceleration at time t for 
t > 0 is given by a(t) = 3
2t .  When t = 1, the position of the particle is 6 and the 
velocity is 2. 
 
a.  Write an equation for the velocity, v(t), of the particle for all t > 0. 
 
b.  Write an equation for the position, x(t), of the particle for all t > 0. 
 
c.  Find the position of the particle when t = e.

questions.prompt:
A ladder 15 feet long is leaning against a building so that the end X is on level 
ground and end Y is on the wall.  X is moved away from the building at the 
constant rate of 1
2 foot per second.   
a. Find the rate in feet per second at which the length OY is changing when 
X is 9 feet from the building. 
b. Find the rate of change in square feet per second of the area of the 
triangle XOY when X is 9 feet from the building.
